items.en: Comparing two games: Given a question, we can play two debates so that Alice and Bob can be both first and second player. The human then judges which agent performed better overall. This game is again manifestly symmetric but requires the human to absorb and judge twice as much information.
items.zh: 比较两个游戏：给定一个问题，我们可以进行两场辩论，使得Alice和Bob分别担任先手和后手。然后人类判断哪个智能体整体表现更好。这个游戏再次明显对称，但要求人类吸收和判断两倍的信息。

items.en: Komi: In Go, the second player gets 6.56.56.5 extra points to counter the first player advantage. We could use the same mechanism for debate if the human judges are able to produce smooth scores rather than binary win/loss decisions, though it is unclear how to ask for such scores. Noisy binary judgements can also act as smooth scores, as long as there is enough noise relative to the first player advantage.
items.zh: 贴目：在围棋中，后手获得6.5个额外点数以抵消先手优势。如果人类裁判能够产生平滑的分数而不是二元胜负决策，我们可以对辩论使用相同的机制，尽管如何要求这样的分数尚不清楚。有噪声的二元判断也可以充当平滑分数，只要噪声相对于先手优势足够大。

items.en: In a typical game of chess or Go, the two players have perfect information about the board but do not have access to each other’s thoughts. Since the players have limited compute, we have some of the character of an imperfect information game, which is unfortunate if the goal is perfect play.
items.zh: 在典型的国际象棋或围棋游戏中，两个玩家对棋盘有完全信息，但无法访问对方的想法。由于玩家算力有限，我们具有不完全信息游戏的一些特征，如果目标是完美玩法，这很不幸。

items.en: We can rectify this problem in debate by giving each agent access to the activations of the other agent at previous time steps, so that the two agents become one agent competing against itself. Note that activations from previous steps should be treated as constant features: we do not want Alice to adjust their thoughts to adversarially change Bob’s actions via backpropagation.
items.zh: 我们可以通过让每个智能体访问另一个智能体在先前时间步的激活值来纠正辩论中的这个问题，从而使两个智能体变成一个与自己竞争的智能体。注意，先前时间步的激活值应被视为常数特征：我们不希望Alice调整其想法以通过反向传播对手性地改变Bob的行动。

items.en: If we believe perfect play produces aligned behavior, sharing activations should make approximate perfect play easier to learn. In particular, shared activations make intentional lies trivial to discover if not to refute. The theoretical picture is the same, since knowing thoughts does not change the perfect information setting for agents with unbounded compute.
items.zh: 如果我们相信完美玩法会产生对齐行为，共享激活值应该使近似完美玩法更容易学习。特别是，共享激活值使得故意撒谎即使无法反驳也容易被发现。理论图景相同，因为对于算力无限的智能体，知道想法不会改变完全信息设置。

items.en: Instead of two agents, we could treat each step of the debate as separate agents trying to convince the human that their statements are useful. To remain zero sum, we let a pair of agents AlicensubscriptAlice𝑛\textrm{Alice}_{n} and BobnsubscriptBob𝑛\textrm{Bob}_{n} make statements an,bnsubscript𝑎𝑛subscript𝑏𝑛a_{n},b_{n} at step n𝑛n. The judge sees all pairs (a0,b0),…,(an−1,bn−1)subscript𝑎0subscript𝑏0…subscript𝑎𝑛1subscript𝑏𝑛1(a_{0},b_{0}),\ldots,(a_{n-1},b_{n-1}) and judges who won each pair. The advantage of many agents is that Alice7subscriptAlice7\textrm{Alice}_{7} is not motivated to defend a lie or unintentional misstatement by Alice2subscriptAlice2\textrm{Alice}_{2}.
items.zh: 我们可以不采用两个智能体，而是将辩论的每一步视为独立的智能体，试图说服人类其陈述有用。为了保持零和，我们让一对智能体Alice_n和Bob_n在第n步做出陈述a_n, b_n。裁判看到所有对(a_0, b_0), ..., (a_{n-1}, b_{n-1})，并判断每对中谁获胜。多智能体的优势在于，Alice_7没有动机去为Alice_2的谎言或无意错误陈述辩护。

items.en: A difficulty with many agents is that the human must be able to judge whether a statement in the middle of a debate is good or bad, which may be much harder than judging an overall debate. For example, it is easy for an amateur Go player to decide who won a game of Go, but hard to know which particular moves were mistakes even once the whole game is known. The theory of many player games is also more complicated: solving tabular n>2𝑛2n>2 player games requires the larger complexity class \PPAD\PPAD\PPAD vs. ¶¶\P for two player zero sum. Although algorithms exist to train agents in n>2𝑛2n>2 player environments [Lowe et al., 2017b], the most impressive results have come from zero sum self play.
items.zh: 多智能体的一个困难是，人类必须能够判断辩论中途的陈述是好是坏，这可能比判断整个辩论困难得多。例如，业余围棋棋手很容易判断谁赢了围棋比赛，但即使知道整盘棋，也很难知道哪些特定走法是错误的。多人博弈的理论也更复杂：解决表格化n>2玩家博弈需要更大的复杂度类\PPAD\PPAD\PPAD，而两人零和博弈是¶\P。尽管存在训练n>2玩家环境中智能体的算法[Lowe et al., 2017b]，最令人印象深刻的结果来自零和自我对弈。

items.en: If the difficulties of training and judging the n𝑛n player case are resolved, however, it may prove a stronger model than two players. In particular, any two player debate protocol can be simulated by the n𝑛n player pairwise model by giving all AlicensubscriptAlice𝑛\textrm{Alice}_{n} the same score, but we do not know of a natural way to simulate the general n𝑛n player protocol with two players.
items.zh: 然而，如果训练和判断n玩家案例的困难得到解决，它可能比两人模型更强大。特别是，任何两人辩论协议都可以通过给所有Alice_n相同分数来用n玩家成对模型模拟，但我们不知道用两人玩家自然模拟一般n玩家协议的方法。

items.en: In Go, a player who believes they are behind is motivated to increase the variance of the game, for example by starting a large fight. Such an action makes the expected final score worse, but increases the tail probability of winning. Variance increase is rational because only winning or losing matters, not the specific score. We would expect the same effect in a debate game with a binary win/loss decision. Variance may be beneficial: a losing agent may search for subtle arguments which have some chance of proving important and winning. However, misleading or lying may also increase variance, which would be bad.
items.zh: 在围棋中，认为自己落后的玩家有动机增加游戏的方差，例如通过发起大规模战斗。这样的行动会使期望最终得分变差，但会增加获胜的尾部概率。方差增加是理性的，因为只有输赢重要，具体得分不重要。我们预期在具有二元胜负决策的辩论游戏中会有同样的效果。方差可能是有益的：落后的智能体可能会寻找微妙的论点，这些论点有一定机会证明重要并获胜。然而，误导或撒谎也可能增加方差，这将是不利的。

items.en: It may be possible to penalize variance increasing strategies by adjusting the payoff structure, at the cost of requiring more information from the judge. For example, the judge could award Alice any score a∈[−1,1]𝑎11a\in[-1,1], and give Bob b=−a𝑏𝑎b=-a. A move that increases the variance but also worsens the mean would be penalized on average.
items.zh: 通过调整收益结构来惩罚增加方差的策略是可能的，但代价是需要裁判提供更多信息。例如，裁判可以给Alice任意分数a∈[-1,1]，并给Bob b=-a。增加方差但降低均值的移动平均而言会受到惩罚。

items.en: Debate is closely related to Christiano et al. [2018]’s amplification model of AI alignment. Indeed, we found debate while trying to better understand amplification. We briefly summarize amplification, then describe how it relates to debate.
items.zh: 辩论与Christiano等人[2018]的AI对齐放大模型密切相关。事实上，我们在试图更好地理解放大时发现了辩论。我们简要总结放大，然后描述它与辩论的关系。

items.en: Amplification is also framed in terms of an ML question/answering system, with the goal of answering questions which are beyond human ability to directly answer. However, we assume that even if the human cannot answer a question Q𝑄Q, they can decompose the question into subquestions qksubscript𝑞𝑘q_{k} such that if they knew the answers aksubscript𝑎𝑘a_{k} to the subquestions they could reconstruct the original answer A𝐴A. The ML system is called recursively on qksubscript𝑞𝑘q_{k} to produce answers aksubscript𝑎𝑘a_{k}, the human reconstructs A𝐴A, and we train the system further on (Q,A)𝑄𝐴(Q,A). Since human time is precious, we additionally train modules to produce subquestions and reconstruct answers, for a total of three modules:
items.zh: 放大同样以ML问答系统为框架，目标是回答人类无法直接回答的问题。然而，我们假设即使人类无法直接回答问题Q，他们也能将问题分解为子问题q_k，使得如果知道子问题的答案a_k，就能重构原始答案A。ML系统被递归调用以产生答案a_k，人类重构A，然后我们在(Q,A)上进一步训练系统。由于人类时间宝贵，我们还训练模块来产生子问题和重构答案，总共三个模块：

items.en: Answerer: Takes questions Q𝑄Q to answers A𝐴A.
items.zh: 回答者：将问题Q映射到答案A。

items.en: Questioner: Takes questions Q𝑄Q to subquestions qksubscript𝑞𝑘q_{k}.
items.zh: 提问者：将问题Q映射到子问题q_k。

items.en: Checker: Takes (Q,qk,ak)𝑄subscript𝑞𝑘subscript𝑎𝑘(Q,q_{k},a_{k}) to answers A𝐴A.
items.zh: 检查者：将(Q, q_k, a_k)映射到答案A。

items.en: All three components are trained by supervised learning. In practice questioning and checking can be interleaved, so that e.g. q2subscript𝑞2q_{2} could depend on a1subscript𝑎1a_{1}, but this does not affect the discussion here.555Christiano et al. [2018] treat the questioner and checker as a single combined module. We separate them in order to discuss adversarial training of the questioner while still using supervised learning for the checker.
items.zh: 所有三个组件都通过监督学习训练。在实践中，提问和检查可以交错进行，例如q_2可能依赖于a_1，但这不影响这里的讨论。5Christiano等人[2018]将提问者和检查者视为一个组合模块。我们将其分开，以便在讨论提问者的对抗训练时仍对检查者使用监督学习。

items.en: Debate: Two agents alternate in an adversarial setting to convince a human judge.
items.zh: 辩论：两个智能体在对抗环境中交替说服人类裁判。

items.en: Amplification: One agent is trained on a human combining recursive calls to the agent.
items.zh: 放大：一个智能体通过人类组合对智能体的递归调用来训练。

items.en: Viewed from a complexity theory perspective, these match two different definitions of \PSPACE\PSPACE\PSPACE:
items.zh: 从复杂性理论的角度看，这两种模型对应于PSPACE的两种不同定义：

items.en: \PSPACE=\PSPACEabsent\PSPACE= polynomial length adversarial games.
items.zh: PSPACE = 多项式长度的对抗游戏。

items.en: \PSPACE=\PSPACEabsent\PSPACE= polynomial depth recursion.
items.zh: PSPACE = 多项式深度的递归。

items.en: Thus we expect the models to have similar capabilities, at least in theory. Both models are framed in terms of recursive computations over trees, and thus can benefit from AlphaZero-style iteration.
items.zh: 因此我们期望这些模型具有相似的能力，至少在理论上是这样。两种模型都以树上的递归计算为框架，因此可以从AlphaZero风格的迭代中受益。

items.en: The equivalence becomes concrete if we contrast the three modules in the debate model (the two debaters and the judge) with the three modules in the amplification model (Answerer, Questioner, and Checker). The Answerer is analogous to one of the debaters and the Checker is analogous to the judge, but the Questioner differs from a debater in that it is trained via supervised learning on human subquestions rather than adversarially against the Answerer. Thus, debate has two powerful agents engaged in self play to explain things to a human or human surrogate judge. Amplification has one powerful agent trained with the help of two human surrogates. Nevertheless, some small changes can bring the models closer together:
items.zh: 如果我们对比辩论模型中的三个模块（两个辩论者和裁判）与放大模型中的三个模块（回答者、提问者和检查者），这种等价性就变得具体了。回答者类似于一个辩论者，检查者类似于裁判，但提问者与辩论者的不同之处在于，它是通过人类子问题的监督学习而非与回答者的对抗训练来训练的。因此，辩论有两个强大的智能体进行自我对弈，向人类或人类代理裁判解释事物。放大有一个强大的智能体，借助两个人类代理进行训练。尽管如此，一些小的改变可以使模型更接近：

items.en: We can move amplification closer to debate (and gain the self play property) by training the Questioner adversarially to help the Checker reveal inconsistencies in the Answerer’s answers.
items.zh: 我们可以通过对抗性训练提问者来帮助检查者揭示回答者答案中的不一致性，从而使放大更接近辩论（并获得自我对弈特性）。

items.en: We can move debate closer to amplification by training debaters on statements provided by humans, corresponding to injecting demonstrations into RL.
items.zh: 我们可以通过训练辩论者使用人类提供的陈述（相当于向RL注入示范）来使辩论更接近放大。

items.en: The equivalence is far from exact: the feedback for a debate is about the whole game and the feedback for amplification is per step, debate as presented uses reinforcement learning while the easiest versions of amplification use supervised learning, and so on. However all these features can be adjusted in either direction.
items.zh: 这种等价性远非精确：辩论的反馈是关于整个游戏的，而放大的反馈是每步的；辩论使用强化学习，而最简单的放大版本使用监督学习，等等。然而所有这些特征都可以向任一方向调整。

items.en: Writing the equivalence in terms of Alice/Bob vs. Answer/Questioner highlights an apparent advantage of the debate model: it works even if it is superhumanly difficult to generate subquestions that are strong enough consistency checks. In particular, this could happen if the branching factor for potentially relevant subquestions is too high. The ability for debate to handle high branching factor means that shallow debate is more powerful than shallow amplification: a debate about the best vacation can have depth two if the human can compare pairs of locations, while an amplification tree necessarily has log\log depth in the number of locations. The advantage goes away if we inject self play into amplification by adversarially training the Questioner.
items.zh: 用Alice/Bob与回答者/提问者的对比来表述等价性，凸显了辩论模型的一个明显优势：即使生成足够强的子问题作为一致性检查是超人类难度的，它仍然有效。特别是，当潜在相关子问题的分支因子过高时，这种情况可能发生。辩论处理高分支因子的能力意味着浅层辩论比浅层放大更强大：关于最佳假期的辩论深度可以为二（如果人类能比较成对地点），而放大树的深度必然为地点数量的对数。如果我们通过对抗性训练提问者将自我对弈注入放大，这一优势就会消失。

items.en: The argument also shows that the assumptions each model makes about humans are similar. Debate assumes lying is harder than arguing against a lie given a human judge, while stock amplification assumes that lying is harder than questioning a lie given a human judge and a human questioner. The human questioner limit goes away once we inject self play into amplification.
items.zh: 该论证还表明，每个模型对人类所做的假设是相似的。辩论假设说谎比在人类裁判面前反驳谎言更难，而标准放大假设说谎比在人类裁判和人类提问者面前质疑谎言更难。一旦我们将自我对弈注入放大，人类提问者的限制就消失了。

items.en: We have described debate as an approach to aligning AI systems stronger than humans, and discussed a variety of theoretical and practical properties of the model. At this point debate is proposal only for the natural language case, and we have demonstrated only a basic experiment for MNIST images. Significant research will be required to know whether debate works in practice with human judges. Much of the required work is empirical, both on the human and ML sides, though we believe further theoretical results are also valuable. Areas for future work include:
items.zh: 我们将辩论描述为一种对齐比人类更强的AI系统的方法，并讨论了该模型的各种理论和实践特性。目前辩论仅针对自然语言案例提出，我们仅对MNIST图像进行了基础实验。要了解辩论是否能在人类裁判实践中有效，还需要大量研究。所需工作大部分是实证性的，涉及人类和ML两方面，尽管我们认为进一步的理论结果也很有价值。未来的工作领域包括：

items.en: Richer theoretical models: Our discussion of \DEBATE=\PSPACE\DEBATE\PSPACE\DEBATE=\PSPACE in the complexity theory setting leaves out many important considerations. In particular, judges are modeled as limited but free of error, and agents have unlimited computational power. More refined theoretical models may be able to probe the properties of debate more closely, and act as a complement to experimental results.
items.zh: 更丰富的理论模型：我们在复杂性理论背景下对辩论的讨论忽略了许多重要因素。特别是，法官被建模为能力有限但无错误，而智能体拥有无限的计算能力。更精细的理论模型或许能更深入地探究辩论的特性，并作为实验结果的补充。

items.en: Human experiments that test value judgement: Does debate with human judges produce aligned behavior even in situations involving moral questions where the judge is biased? We believe it is possible to test this question without waiting for general dialog agents, and in particular that debate may be applicable to fairness and bias questions.
items.zh: 测试价值判断的人类实验：在涉及道德问题且法官存在偏见的情况下，辩论能否通过人类法官产生对齐行为？我们认为，无需等待通用对话智能体即可测试这一问题，并且辩论尤其可能适用于公平性和偏见问题。

items.en: ML experiments that approximate the human aspects of debate: Strong self play results already exist in a variety of games, but there is no theory that says self play works for all games. In the near term, we would like games without the complexities of natural language that approximate properties of human judges. The sparse MNIST classifier experiment of Section 3.1 is one example; we would like others.
items.zh: 近似人类辩论方面的机器学习实验：强自我对弈结果已在多种游戏中存在，但没有理论表明自我对弈适用于所有游戏。短期内，我们希望设计一些没有自然语言复杂性但能近似人类法官特性的游戏。第3.1节的稀疏MNIST分类器实验是一个例子；我们还需要其他例子。

items.en: Natural language debate: As soon as possible, we want to test debate in the natural language setting with real humans. Even if this is difficult in the case of unrestricted dialog, it may be possible to construct narrower dialog environments that capture more of the flavor of debate and remain tractable for modern ML.
items.zh: 自然语言辩论：我们希望尽快在自然语言环境中用真实人类测试辩论。即使在无限制对话的情况下难以实现，也可能构建更狭窄的对话环境，以捕捉更多辩论的特点，并使其对现代机器学习而言易于处理。

items.en: Interaction between debate and other safety methods: Debate does not address other safety concerns such as robustness to adversarial examples, distributional shift, or safe exploration. In particular, the training process for debate could be unsafe even if the final equilibrium is aligned. We believe other algorithms will be required alongside debate or similar for a complete solution to safety, and it is important to know how the various pieces interact.
items.zh: 辩论与其他安全方法的交互：辩论并未解决其他安全问题，如对抗样本的鲁棒性、分布偏移或安全探索。特别是，辩论的训练过程可能不安全，即使最终均衡是对齐的。我们认为，要实现完整的安全解决方案，需要将其他算法与辩论或类似方法结合使用，并且了解各部分如何交互至关重要。

items.en: More broadly, we now have two proposals for aligning strong agents based on human preferences: amplification and debate. If there are two there are likely more, especially as amplification and debate are sufficiently similar that properties of one can be moved across to the other. We encourage the reader to search for others.
items.zh: 更广泛地说，我们现在有两种基于人类偏好对齐强智能体的方案：放大和辩论。既然有两种，很可能还有更多，尤其是因为放大和辩论足够相似，以至于一种方案的特性可以迁移到另一种方案。我们鼓励读者寻找其他方案。

items.en: We thank Jan Leike, Rohin Shah, and Victoria Krakovna for comments on initial versions of debate, Joshua Achiam, Chris Olah, and Dylan Hadfield-Manell for help with experiment design, and Catherine Olsson and Julia Galef for helpful conversations about belief bias. John Schulman and Harri Edwards gave detailed comments on the paper, including suggestions for structural changes. Michael Page, Elena Chatziathanasiadou, and Alex Ray played human-only versions of debate informally. We had many useful discussions at an AI strategy retreat run by the Future of Humanity Institute in January 2018, in particular with David Manley. The debate website was built by Robert Lord (https://lord.io).
items.zh: 我们感谢Jan Leike、Rohin Shah和Victoria Krakovna对辩论早期版本的评论，感谢Joshua Achiam、Chris Olah和Dylan Hadfield-Manell在实验设计上的帮助，以及Catherine Olsson和Julia Galef关于信念偏见的有益对话。John Schulman和Harri Edwards对论文提供了详细评论，包括结构修改建议。Michael Page、Elena Chatziathanasiadou和Alex Ray非正式地参与了仅人类的辩论版本。我们在2018年1月由未来人类研究所组织的人工智能战略务虚会上进行了许多有益的讨论，特别是与David Manley的讨论。辩论网站由Robert Lord构建（https://lord.io）。
